note: WO-0073 review candidate only: built through the MapVision/TileAssembly compiler pipeline (WO-0071/0072), not hand-authored tiles. Production Map001 not overwritten; installation requires Chris's explicit acceptance.

parameters: Atlas blueprint anchor EVT-HOM-003
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-003: Child Near Old Panel
This metal wall hums if you press your ear to it.
Mama says not to do that, so I only do it
when she's looking away.
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-003: Child Near Old Panel STATE_02_AFTER_TREMOR
The wall hummed by itself today.
I didn't even touch it.
Atlas blueprint anchor EVT-HOM-004
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-004: Farmer With Warm Stones
These warm stones keep the seedlings alive
through cold nights.
My grandfather said they were a gift
from the deep earth.
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-004: Farmer With Warm Stones STATE_03_AFTER_SWORD
The warm stones went cold for a breath,
then came back hotter than before.
Can't say I liked that.
Atlas blueprint anchor EVT-HOM-005
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-005: Skyreach Joker
Skyreach Hill?
Sure, go on up. Wave to the curse for me.
Actually, don't. Curses are terrible at waving back.
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-005: Skyreach Joker STATE_02_ACCESS_OPEN (ATLAS-STY-012 PH-DLG-ASH-JOKER-OPEN)
WO-0048: added missing middle page between intro and after-Sword; ATLAS-STY-012 defines this as a distinct beat
The rope's down and the guards face the other way.
If you're going up that hill, tell the curse I said
nothing. Nothing at all.
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-005: Skyreach Joker STATE_03_AFTER_SWORD
WO-0046: gated on J1_Sword_Obtained (7), not J1_Skyreach_AccessOpen (2), to match dialogue content
You went up the hill and came back glowing.
I went up there once and only came back itchy.
Atlas blueprint anchor EVT-HOM-006
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-006: Dock Messenger
Boats are tied down at Rustshore.
Sea's calm, but the lighthouse keeps
flashing wrong.
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-006: Dock Messenger STATE_04_AFTER_NODE07
WO-0046: gated on J1_Node07_Offline (16) for consistency with Elara/Shopkeeper's After-Node-Seven page
The lighthouse settled down.
Dockmaster says the sea looks open again.
Atlas blueprint anchor EVT-HOM-007
Atlas executable event EVT-HOM-007: Hidden Item
Found an item.
A
Already resolved by Self Switch A.

events.name: Village Elder
events.note: Atlas NPC-ASH-ELD-001; WO-0047 playtest fix: was an inert placeholder, now has real dialogue.

parameters: Atlas blueprint anchor NPC-ASH-ELD-001
ATLAS-STY-010 Village Elder STATE_01_INTRO
Glassfield has always been part of Ashford.
That doesn't mean it belongs to us.
Beyond Rustshore the water keeps going farther than any of us have sailed.
My grandfather always swore there were shores out there fit for a story,
if a body was brave enough to find them.
ATLAS-STY-010 Village Elder STATE_02_AFTER_TREMOR
The old panels keep the north wind out better than timber.
Useful things don't have to be understood, I suppose.
Atlas blueprint anchor INT-ASH-WARM-STONE-VENT
Atlas local examine INT-ASH-WARM-STONE-VENT
Warm stones sit beneath a rusted grate,
faintly hot even on a cold morning.
Atlas blueprint anchor INT-ASH-OLD-PANEL
Atlas local examine INT-ASH-OLD-PANEL
A wall of reused metal panels, faintly warm
to the touch. If you listen closely, it hums.
